articles.title: Maharashtra heatwave update: Yellow alert for Mumbai, Palghar, Thane and Raigad for today as temperature breaches 40 degree mark. Check IMD's prediction
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Maharashtra
articles.full_text:
Listen to this article in summarized format

Yellow alert in Maharashtra for today
A severe heatwave is set to impact parts of Maharashtra on Wednesday, April 15 and the IMD has issued a yellow alert for eight districts across the state. The IMD has also sounded a yellow alert for Mumbai, cautioning residents about a spell of rising temperatures coupled with stifling humidity from April 15 to April 16. The weather office has predicted “hot and humid conditions in isolated pockets” in Mumbai over the next two days. The temperatures for April 14 and April 15 in Mumbai are expected to be between 34°C and 25 °C.ALSO READ: Extreme heatwave alert: Kerala records hazardous UV levels
In the Konkan region, districts including Palghar, Thane, Mumbai, Raigad, Ratnagiri, and Sindhudurg, minimum temperatures are likely to be in the range of 26°C and 28°C, while maximum temperatures could reach 34°C to 36°C.
In Western Maharashtra, Sangli and Solapur are also under a yellow alert. These districts may witness minimum temperatures between 24°C and 26°C, with maximum temperatures soaring to 38°C to 40°C. According to IMD scientist SD Sanap, the temperatures were likely to remain in the 40-41°C range over the next three to five days.
Mumbai temperature: Hot and humid conditions to prevail
The IMD has predicted that Konkan districts including Palghar, Thane, Mumbai, Raigad, Ratnagiri and Sindhudurg might experience hot and humid conditions in isolated pockets between April 14 and 16, even as the region is expected to remain largely dry. Hot and humid weather is very likely over Konkan and Goa from April 14 to 16, over Marathwada on April 14 and 15, and over Madhya Maharashtra on April 17.Hot and humid weather conditions are expected in central Maharashtra between April 15 and 18, in Marathwada between April 16 and 18, in Vidarbha between April 15 and 19, and in Konkan between April 15 and 17 at isolated places.
With temperatures remaining elevated and heatwave conditions expected in parts of the state, authorities have advised residents to avoid prolonged sun exposure, stay well-hydrated, and take necessary precautions, especially during peak afternoon hours.
